query: Johor state election 2026 expected outcome, green wave PN Perikatan Nasional Malay voters, Barisan Nasional incumbency, Onn Hafiz popularity
answer:
I've completed the research on the 2026 Johor state election. Here's a summary of the key findings:

**Bottom line:** Barisan Nasional (BN) is overwhelmingly favoured to retain Johor — Polymarket prices it at ~96%, and analysts call a BN win "almost a foregone conclusion." The only real question is the size of BN's majority.

**On the four factors you asked about:**

1. **Expected outcome:** BN likely to expand its 2022 majority. Ong Kian Ming projects BN could sweep **53 of 56 seats** (up from 40), with PH ~3 and PN wiped out.

2. **Green wave / PN / Malay voters:** The Green Wave was a **northern-peninsular phenomenon** (Kedah, Kelantan, Terengganu, Perlis) — not southern Johor. PN is contesting only 33 seats and is "not in Johor to win," weakened by the **PAS–Bersatu breakdown** and Gerakan's withdrawal. No meaningful green wave is expected; PN risks being shut out entirely.

3. **BN incumbency:** Strong. BN holds a two-thirds majority and framed the poll as a "referendum on continuity, stability and performance." Notably, BN and PH — federal unity-government allies — are contesting all 56 seats against each other, splitting the anti-BN field.

4. **Onn Hafiz popularity:** Cited as **"BN's strongest political asset in Johor,"** with an energetic image resonating with urban/semi-urban voters even as party loyalty weakens.

**Caveats:** Calling polls a year early is a gambit "engendering some risk"; cost-of-living, jobs, and housing grievances could dent BN's margin, and the many multi-cornered contests add some unpredictability.

Full structured findings (12 key facts, 12 sources, 14 data points) have been submitted.

sources.title: 2026 Johor state election - Wikipedia
sources.snippet: Election expected 11 July 2026; dissolution announced 1 June 2026 by Onn Hafiz Ghazi; all 56 seats contested.

sources.title: 2022 Johor state election - Wikipedia
sources.snippet: BN won 40 seats and a two-thirds majority; PH won 12; PN won 3; MUDA 1.

sources.snippet: 172 candidates, 2.72m voters; 14 straight/27 three/12 four/3 five-cornered; PN contesting 33 seats, 'not in Johor to win the state'.

sources.snippet: BN enjoys momentum but the early-poll gambit engenders some risk.

sources.snippet: Onn Hafiz repeatedly named favourably by voters; energetic image resonates with urban/semi-urban voters.

sources.snippet: Johor (11 July) and Negeri Sembilan (1 Aug) polls test Anwar's Madani government.

sources.snippet: GE15 Green Wave concentrated in Perlis, Kedah, Kelantan, Terengganu; produced a hung parliament.

sources.snippet: Bersatu contests majority of PN seats; PAS, Gerakan, MIPP allocated seats; Pejuang added as ally.

sources.snippet: Official 2022 EC tally: BN 40, PH 11, PN 3, PKR 1, MUDA 1.
